raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation targets a research-paper section whose contribution framing and positioning may need adjustment above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The section makes a causal and argumentative chain from method design to gains and bottleneck claims, so logical consistency is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The artifact reports metrics and ablations that are used to support claims about gains and transfer, making evidence-claim alignment central.","R1.5":"Helping with a section naturally includes the ordering and narrative arc across paragraphs and subsections.","R1.6":"A broad request to help with a written section makes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any projection of this broad delegation should preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about gains, transfer, and bottlenecks, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually editing or evaluating the section.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.","RX.5":"Responsibility assignments should be traceable to features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation targets a research-paper section whose contribution framing and positioning may need adjustment above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The section makes a causal and argumentative chain from method design to gains and bottleneck claims, so logical consistency is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports metrics and ablations that are used to support claims about gains and transfer, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.5: Helping with a section naturally includes the ordering and narrative arc across paragraphs and subsections.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to help with a written section makes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation should preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about gains, transfer, and bottlenecks, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually editing or evaluating the section.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility assignments should be traceable to features of the delegation and artifact.
